claimText: The Gaganyaan mission aims to launch a crew of 3 members to a 400 km Low Earth Orbit (LEO) for a 3-day mission.
evidenceExcerpt: Gaganyaan aims to launch a crew of 3 members to a 400 km Low Earth Orbit (LEO) for a 3-day mission.
examUse: Statement-based question on the specific parameters of the Gaganyaan mission.

claimText: The Gaganyaan mission utilizes the Launch Vehicle Mark-3 (LVM-3), which is a 3-stage rocket.
evidenceExcerpt: The mission utilizes the Launch Vehicle Mark-3 (LVM-3), a 3-stage rocket formerly known as GSLV Mk-III.
examUse: Statement-based question on the launch vehicle used for India's human spaceflight.

claimText: Vyommitra is an AI-powered half-humanoid robot that will undertake the first uncrewed mission of the Gaganyaan programme.
evidenceExcerpt: Vyommitra, an AI-powered half-humanoid (gynoid), will undertake the first uncrewed mission of Gaganyaan.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying Vyommitra and its role.
distractorHooks: Part of the crewed mission rather than uncrewed

claimText: A successful Gaganyaan launch will make India the fourth country to launch a crewed spacecraft, after the US, Russia, and China.
evidenceExcerpt: A successful launch will make India the 4th country to launch a crewed spacecraft, following the US, Russia, and China.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's global standing in human spaceflight.
distractorHooks: Includes European Space Agency (ESA) or France

claimText: The Bharatiya Antariksh Station (BAS) is planned to orbit at 400-450 km above the Earth and will consist of five modules.
evidenceExcerpt: Bharatiya Antariksh Station (BAS) is planned to orbit at 400-450 km above the Earth and will consist of five modules.
examUse: Statement-based question on the specifications of India's planned space station.
